name: Spot by NetApp Elastigroup API
what_it_does: Cloud compute cost optimization platform with REST API — enabling DevOps and FinOps teams to automatically provision and scale EC2 instances (AWS Spot, Azure Spot, GCP Preemptible) with intelligent instance diversification across types and AZs, workload-aware interruption handling, and predictive workload analysis for non-Kubernetes compute workloads.
use_cases: Optimizing EC2 instance costs via Elastigroup API for automatic spot instance provisioning
Managing Elastigroup configurations via API for infrastructure-as-code spot compute management
Scaling stateful workloads via Elastigroup API with persistent storage and IP retention
Analyzing cost savings via Elastigroup API for FinOps cost optimization reporting
Integrating Elastigroup with CI/CD via API for dynamic compute configuration in pipelines
not_for: Kubernetes workloads (use Spot Ocean for Kubernetes-native spot instance management)
Stateless workloads that can use K8s (Ocean is better for containerized workloads)
Teams not on AWS, Azure, or GCP (Elastigroup is cloud-specific)
best_when: A DevOps or FinOps team running EC2-based workloads (non-Kubernetes) wants to dramatically reduce compute costs using spot instances with intelligent interruption handling and instance diversification.
avoid_when: You run Kubernetes workloads (use Spot Ocean), need on-premises compute, or have stateless workloads that can easily containerize.

auth.notes: Bearer token authentication. Authorization: Bearer {api_token}. API token from Spot console (spot.io). REST API at api.spotinst.io/aws/ec2/group/ (AWS) or /azure/compute/group/ (Azure). Account ID required: X-Account-ID header. Terraform provider: spotinst/terraform-provider-spotinst. Python SDK: spotinst-sdk2. Webhooks for scale events and interruption notifications. Same Spot.io API infrastructure as Ocean product.

pricing.paid_tiers: Percentage of savings model; typically 20-25% of savings generated
pricing.notes: US company (Tel Aviv origin). Spot.io (formerly Spotinst) acquired by NetApp in 2020 for ~$450M. Elastigroup: EC2/VM-level spot optimization. Ocean: Kubernetes-level (separate product). Stateful nodes: persistent volumes + Elastic IPs preserved across spot interruptions. Intelligent rebalancing: proactively replaces instances before interruption signals. CI/CD integration: Jenkins plugin, Terraform provider, AWS CodePipeline. Savings: 60-80% reduction vs on-demand EC2. Interruption prediction: ML model predicts spot interruptions 30+ minutes in advance. Supported: ECS (ECS clusters), EMR, EKS worker nodes, general EC2.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token + X-Account-ID. Same API as Ocean (different paths). Elastigroup (EC2) vs Ocean (K8s). Terraform primary IaC. Stateful nodes for persistent workloads. NetApp subsidiary. Pay-for-savings model. Interruption prediction ML.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Group reads idempotent by group ID. Configuration updates: idempotent by group ID (PUT). Cost data reads idempotent by date range. Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: X-Account-ID header required — Spot account ID (not AWS account ID)
Elastigroup (EC2) vs Ocean (Kubernetes): different API paths — /aws/ec2/group/ vs /ocean/aws/k8s/
Stateful nodes: extra configuration required for persistent storage (EBS volumes) and Elastic IPs
Spot interruptions: 2-minute warning — configure interruption handlers for graceful shutdown
Savings pricing: 20-25% of savings — validate ROI before contract; large EC2 spend = significant Spot fees
AWS IAM role required: Elastigroup needs IAM permissions to manage EC2 instances on your behalf

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token. SOC2 certified. NetApp parent: enterprise security standards. Spot.io manages AWS IAM role with EC2 permissions — high blast radius; use least-privilege policies. Stateful nodes: EBS volume access requires additional IAM permissions. X-Account-ID separation for account isolation. Webhooks: verify webhook payloads. US data residency — GDPR via DPA. Interruption handling: graceful shutdown scripts have security implications.
